positioning.coreThesis: Stratos is not a website shop. It is a local-business revenue operations layer: premium front-end, conversion paths, AI intake, follow-up, proof, and owner visibility in one operating system.
positioning.buyerPromise: If a lead touches the business through web, phone, form, DM, or after-hours intent, Stratos makes the next step clearer, faster, and less dependent on manual staff bandwidth.
qualityBar: Every surface must answer: what decision does this help Bradley make or what sale does it help close?
Every client-facing claim must be proof-backed, demo-labeled, or explicitly projected.
Every creative asset must be phone-readable, logo-safe, and locally composited when text accuracy matters.
Every recurring task should be script/local-model first before spending premium model calls.

operatingSystem.goal: Create conversations with high-value Boca businesses every week.
operatingSystem.failureMode: Building more assets while no outreach leaves the system.

operatingSystem.goal: Route every prospect into a named package instead of custom chaos.
dailyMoves: Send close-room with one recommended next step
operatingSystem.failureMode: Selling 'websites + AI' vaguely instead of an obvious system.

operatingSystem.goal: Turn demos, audits, screenshots, and client outcomes into reusable trust assets.
operatingSystem.failureMode: Talking about capability without visual evidence.

operatingSystem.goal: Make Stratos look premium enough to justify premium offers.
dailyMoves: Use Higgsfield only for high-leverage key art/video
operatingSystem.failureMode: AI-ish templates, fake text, warped UI, repeated blobs.

operatingSystem.goal: Keep the Vercel/public/static path reliable from locked-down school computers.
operatingSystem.ownerMetric: Main branch deployable at all times
operatingSystem.failureMode: Local-only improvements that never reach the public hub.

triggerSignals: Instagram sends traffic but site does not route consult intent
After-hours visitors get no immediate path
Proof/reviews are not placed near booking decisions
dealPackets.firstTouch: Your site should feel as premium as the treatment room — and it should capture consult intent even after hours.
dealPackets.demoAngle: Treatment recommender + consult capture + proof sequencing + follow-up.
dealPackets.closeRoomPromise: Turn high-intent aesthetic browsing into booked consult requests with a premium front door and immediate follow-up.

triggerSignals: Reviews are passive instead of decision-support
Missed calls can turn into competitors
dealPackets.firstTouch: New patients compare fast — the site should answer objections and make booking obvious before they call another office.
dealPackets.demoAngle: New-patient path + insurance answers + missed-call text-back + review engine.
dealPackets.closeRoomPromise: Capture more new-patient demand with clearer service paths and immediate follow-up.

triggerSignals: Seasonal tune-up opportunities are not nurtured
dealPackets.firstTouch: For urgent jobs, the first company to respond clearly usually wins.
dealPackets.demoAngle: Emergency CTA + missed-call recovery + quote intake + maintenance nurture.
dealPackets.closeRoomPromise: Recover more emergency and estimate demand before it leaks to faster competitors.

triggerSignals: Consult requests depend on slow manual follow-up
dealPackets.firstTouch: People with urgent legal questions need a clear intake path, not a generic contact page.
dealPackets.demoAngle: Case routing + urgency capture + trust sequencing + consult request follow-up.
dealPackets.closeRoomPromise: Make high-intent case intake clearer, faster, and easier to respond to.

rules: Demo concepts can show workflow value but cannot imply client performance.
Projected upside must be framed as opportunity math, not guaranteed revenue.
Real client results need source date, screenshot/file, and context before use in marketing.
Outbound proof should start with before/after process, not unsupported numeric claims.

higgsfieldStudio.currentStatus: Authenticated but credit-blocked at last check: free plan, 0 credits. Do not promise new renders until credits are available.
bestUseWhenCreditsReturn: Generate 3 reusable cinematic key-art backgrounds for medspa/dental/home-service campaigns.
Generate 2 vertical Marketing Studio UGC presenter ads for Stratos itself.
Generate 1 polished TV-spot style Stratos brand video.
Avoid generated text/logo in final frames; composite final Stratos logo and captions locally.

promptBank.name: Medspa front desk key art
promptBank.prompt: Premium editorial ad key art for Stratos AI, boutique medspa front desk after hours, elegant dark forest-green and warm-white palette, a phone showing a consult request routed into an AI concierge workflow, cinematic lighting, realistic luxury clinic details, no fake logos, no readable tiny text, high-end agency aesthetic, space for headline overlay.

promptBank.name: Disconnected systems brand visual
promptBank.prompt: Premium B2B operations visual for Stratos AI: fragmented local business tools represented as separate dark glass panels slowly connecting into one clean command flow, forest green signal lines, black and off-white palette, no clutter, no fake brand marks, dramatic but minimal, room for typography overlay.

promptBank.name: UGC founder pitch
promptBank.prompt: A confident young agency founder explains that most local businesses do not need another generic website; they need their website, missed calls, forms, and follow-up connected into one revenue system. Premium but natural, direct-to-camera, Boca Raton local-business context, clear CTA: DM 'front desk' for a quick audit.

promptBank.name: TV spot: website is the front desk
promptBank.prompt: Polished commercial for Stratos AI showing a local business losing after-hours leads, then switching to a premium website plus AI intake and follow-up system. Modern forest-green/black/white brand mood, serious, high-trust, no hype, end with: Your website should act like your best operator.

repoAuditFindings: The project has strong static/Vercel bones: data, demos, close rooms, pitch kits, public export, usage saver, and cron-ready docs.
The weakest area is perceived premium quality: too many surfaces describe systems instead of showing polished boardroom-grade flows.
The next quality jump should be fewer better modules, stronger visual hierarchy, and proof/creative operations that enforce QA before assets ship.
Higgsfield is installed and authenticated, but credits are currently 0; the repo now needs a ready-to-run creative studio rather than pretending renders are available.
